prior to the [1991 general election](/wiki/1991_Saskatchewan_general_election "1991 Saskatchewan general election"), when it adopted its current name.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Provincial electoral district in Saskatchewan, Canada

Moose Jaw Wakamow

| Saskatchewan [electoral district](/wiki/Electoral_district_(Canada) "Electoral district (Canada)") | |

**Moose Jaw Wakamow** is a [provincial](/wiki/Provinces_and_territories_of_Canada "Provinces and territories of Canada") [electoral district](/wiki/Electoral_district_(Canada) "Electoral district (Canada)") for the [Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan "Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan"), Canada. One of two provincial constituencies for the city of [Moose Jaw](/wiki/Moose_Jaw "Moose Jaw"), the riding contains the area of the city south of Caribou Street, northeast of 9th Avenue, and northwest of Thatcher Drive.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Moose_Jaw_Wakamow&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

This district (along with its counterpart [Moose Jaw North](/wiki/Moose_Jaw_North "Moose Jaw North")) was created for the [1967 election](/wiki/1967_Saskatchewan_general_election "1967 Saskatchewan general election") after the Saskatchewan government decided to retire a system of [multiple-MLA electoral divisions](/wiki/Multiple_non-transferable_vote "Multiple non-transferable vote") for the cities of [Regina](/wiki/Regina_City_(provincial_electoral_district) "Regina City (provincial electoral district)"), [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon_City_(provincial_electoral_district) "Saskatoon City (provincial electoral district)"), and [Moose Jaw](/wiki/Moose_Jaw_City "Moose Jaw City"). The riding was called **Moose Jaw South** prior to the [1991 general election](/wiki/1991_Saskatchewan_general_election "1991 Saskatchewan general election"), when it adopted its current name.

From the [2003 election](/wiki/2003_Saskatchewan_general_election "2003 Saskatchewan general election") through the [2011 election](/wiki/2011_Saskatchewan_general_election "2011 Saskatchewan general election"), the riding contained a sizable rural area to the south of Moose Jaw including the [Rural Municipality of Baildon](/wiki/Rural_Municipality_of_Baildon_No._131 "Rural Municipality of Baildon No. 131") and the northern half of the [Rural Municipality of Terrell](/wiki/Rural_Municipality_of_Terrell_No._101 "Rural Municipality of Terrell No. 101"). This rural territory was moved to [Lumsden-Morse](/wiki/Lumsden-Morse "Lumsden-Morse") for the [2016 election](/wiki/2016_Saskatchewan_general_election "2016 Saskatchewan general election"), making Moose Jaw Wakamow back into a strictly urban riding.

For the [2024 general election](/wiki/2024_Saskatchewan_general_election "2024 Saskatchewan general election"), the riding gained the part of the [Rural Municipality of Moose Jaw](/wiki/Rural_Municipality_of_Moose_Jaw_No._161 "Rural Municipality of Moose Jaw No. 161") south of the [Trans-Canada Highway](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Highway_1 "Saskatchewan Highway 1"), including [CFB Moose Jaw](/wiki/CFB_Moose_Jaw "CFB Moose Jaw").[[1]](#cite_note-1)
